_comment: Example configuration file for Power BI Refresh Script
_comment_usage: Copy this file to config.json and fill in your actual values
_comment_env_syntax: You can use ${VAR_NAME} syntax to reference environment variables in any string field

azure._comment_env_vars: You can reference environment variables using ${VAR_NAME} syntax
azure._comment_tenant_id: Your Azure AD tenant ID (GUID format). Example: 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc
azure._example_client_id: Get from Azure Portal > App Registrations > Your App > Application (client) ID
azure._comment_client_secret: Service principal client secret - IMPORTANT: Store securely!
azure._example_client_secret: Get from Azure Portal > App Registrations > Your App > Certificates & secrets

powerbi._comment: Power BI workspace and dataset configuration

workspaces._comment_id: Workspace GUID - get from Power BI workspace URL
workspaces._comment_datasets: List of dataset GUIDs to refresh in this workspace (leave empty to refresh all)

powerbi._comment_workspaces: List of workspaces containing datasets to refresh

execution._comment_poll_interval: Seconds between status checks during refresh (default: 30)
execution._comment_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait for a refresh to complete (default: 3600 = 1 hour)
execution._comment_max_retries: Maximum number of retry attempts for transient errors (default: 3)
execution._comment_retry_backoff: Seconds to wait between retry attempts (exponential backoff)

logging._comment_level: Log level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL (default: INFO)
logging._comment_file: Path to log file (optional, omit to log only to console)

_environment_variables._comment: You can also use environment variables instead of or in addition to this file
_environment_variables._comment_precedence: Precedence: CLI arguments > config file > environment variables
